Title: Natural variation in flavonol accumulation in Arabidopsis is determined by the flavonol glucosyltransferase BGLU6

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native genomic BGLU6_Ler promoter (~1217 bp upstream) for complementation in Arabidopsis Col-0; CaMV 35S promoter for transient GFP fusion localization assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Ler, Col-0, Ws-4; survey across 81 natural accessions from the Nordborg collection
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonol glycosylation within the flavonoid/phenylpropanoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use functional BGLU6 alleles to introduce accession-specific flavonol diglucosides such as F3GG7R into Arabidopsis or related systems; suitable for metabolic engineering of flavonol decoration rather than bulk pathway flux. Best deployed with upstream flavonol-glycoside pathway capacity already present (for example genes producing F3G7R substrate). Natural loss-of-function alleles should be screened before association studies or engineering. Because donor sugar identity remains unresolved and biological benefit is unclear, stacking with precursor-forming genes and cautious phenotyping under UV/stress contexts are recommended.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Most likely transfers a second glucose onto the 3-O-linked glucose of flavonol 3-O-glucoside 7-O-rhamnoside (F3G7R), forming flavonol 3-O-gentiobioside 7-O-rhamnoside (F3GG7R) with a 1→6 interglycosidic linkage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional BGLU6 is essential for accumulation of the accession-specific flavonol glycosides K3GG7R, Q3GG7R, and I3GG7R/F3GG7R; loss-of-function alleles abolish F3GG7R production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Natural variation mapping (RIL linkage plus GWAS); two independent insertion mutants (bglu6-1, bglu6-2) showing loss of F3GG7R; genomic complementation of Col-0 with Ler BGLU6 restoring F3GG7R; metabolite profiling by HPTLC and LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.EC_Number: Not reported; exact donor substrate unresolved, so EC assignment not established confidently
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light-grown seedlings on 0.5 MS with 1.5% sucrose; 16 h light at 21 °C/18.5 °C; organ/developmental comparisons between seedlings and adult plants; no clear correlation with native-site UV index or altitude
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in seedlings; detectable across seedling organs (cotyledon, hypocotyl, root) and lower in adult organs; RNA-seq across accessions showed reduced expression in alleles carrying the premature stop codon
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in seedlings; lower in adult organs
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: No direct physical interactors validated; co-expressed with TT6/F3H; UGT78D2; UGT89C1; 4CL3; UGT75C1; CHI-like; UF3GT; TT19/GST26; TT5/CHI; FLS1; TT3/DFR; BRT1/UGT84A2; UGT78D1; TT4/CHS
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome-wide association mapping using ~206,000 SNPs via GWAPP; RNA-seq accession dataset from Gan et al. 2011 used for expression comparisons (no accession number reported); LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BGLU6 encodes a glycoside hydrolase family 1 protein rather than a canonical UDP-sugar-dependent UGT. Donor sugar remains unresolved; authors hypothesize an acyl-glucose donor similar to anthocyanin acyl-glucose-dependent glucosyltransferases. In vitro enzyme assays and recombinant protein characterization failed, so catalytic assignment is inferred from genetics and metabolite restoration. Several natural loss-of-function alleles occur, including a common premature stop codon at CDS position 1138 in Col-0.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No clear growth penalty or yield effect reported; biological function of F3GG7R remains unknown and the metabolite appears dispensable in many accessions
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Flavonol 3-O-gentiobioside 7-O-rhamnoside (including kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhamnetin derivatives: K3GG7R, Q3GG7R, I3GG7R)
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycoside hydrolase family 1 (GH1); homologous to acyl-glucose-dependent anthocyanin glucosyltransferases; contains atypical catalytic motif TINEG and conserved second GH1-like motif
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant for accession-specific F3GG7R accumulation; essential step, but not established as overall pathway rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Ler (functional allele), with comparison to Col-0, Ws-4 and multiple natural accessions
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm (suggested by transient GFP fusions in BY2 cells)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Linkage mapping and GWAS identified At1g60270/BGLU6 as the causal locus for natural qualitative variation in F3GG7R accumulation. 2. Independent insertion mutants in Ws-4 and Ler lost F3GG7R, and genomic Ler BGLU6 complemented Col-0 to restore F3GG7R. 3. BGLU6 encodes an atypical GH1-type flavonol glucosyltransferase, likely catalyzing formation of flavonol 3-O-gentiobioside 7-O-rhamnoside in seedlings.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seedlings broadly (cotyledon, hypocotyl, root); low levels also in adult organs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely contributes to UV protection and adaptation as a glycosylated flavonol, though specific biological function was not demonstrated

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter used for transient effector expression of MYB12/PFG1, MYB11/PFG2, and MYB111/PFG3 in protoplast co-transfection assays; BGLU6_Ler promoter (~1.2 kb) used as reporter
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: At7 protoplast system; general Arabidopsis context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: These regulators are useful for boosting upstream flavonol-pathway capacity, but this paper shows they are not sufficient to activate BGLU6 directly. For engineering F3GG7R accumulation, combine PFG regulators with a functional BGLU6 allele and other flavonol glycosylation genes rather than relying on PFG activation alone.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Known flavonol-branch activators in Arabidopsis, but in this study they did not activate the BGLU6 promoter in transient assays, indicating no direct control of the BGLU6-dependent F3GG7R step under the tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-transfection assay in Arabidopsis At7 protoplasts using 35S-driven effector constructs and a BGLU6 promoter-GUS reporter; promoter motif prediction with NewPLACE
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Conditionally relevant upstream flavonol-pathway activators, but not decisive direct regulators of BGLU6/F3GG7R formation in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in At7 hypocotyl-derived protoplasts; no external stress treatment specified
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: MYB12/PFG1, MYB11/PFG2, MYB111/PFG3 (accession numbers not provided in the paper text)
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Known to regulate 4CL; CHS; CHI; F3H; FLS; UGT78D1; UGT89C1 (cited background); no direct protein complex tested here
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of flavonol branch biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: None specific for these regulators in this study
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although predictive MYB-binding sites were found in the BGLU6 promoter of multiple accessions, none of the three PFG MYBs activated the BGLU6 promoter in protoplast assays. Authors note that in planta activation cannot be fully excluded because of assay limitations or missing cofactors.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Known flavonol biosynthetic genes such as 4CL, CHS, CHI, F3H, FLS, UGT78D1, UGT89C1; BGLU6 was tested but not supported as a direct target in this study
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factors; PFG1-3 flavonol-branch regulators
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of flavonol biosynthetic genes; no activation of BGLU6 promoter detected here
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activate multiple flavonol biosynthetic and glycosylation genes in the pathway, but failed to activate BGLU6 in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (inferred for MYB transcription factors; not tested in this paper)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB12, MYB11, and MYB111 are discussed as flavonol branch-specific activators that regulate many upstream flavonol genes. 2. Putative MYB sites exist in the BGLU6 promoter, but none of the three MYBs activated the BGLU6 promoter in At7 protoplast co-transfection assays. 3. Therefore BGLU6 regulation differs from classic PFG-controlled flavonol genes, at least in the assay used.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Developmental flavonol-program inputs; not specifically defined in this paper
